name: web-aura-starter
summary: Server-rendered web app wired to the Aura design language: base app-shell, pages/ + fragments/ layout, Aura consumed as a vendor.toml dep (synced via sync-deps) at vendor/aura.

aura.note: Aura ships the framework-neutral artifacts (rhohn94/design-language#26). This template references them at vendor/aura and copies no upstream code. The default consumption mechanism is a vendor.toml dep synced via sync-deps (channel stable, kind asset-bundle, dest vendor/aura); git submodule and static/aura + build step are recorded alternatives. vendor/aura/SRI.txt is an additional browser-SRI asset (per-file subresource-integrity hashes), not the dependency pin. Where an artifact is not yet published, the scaffolded payloads point at its intended path; see docs/web-app-aura-adoption-guide.md.

post-apply-notes: Add the [deps.aura] entry to vendor.toml (repo rhohn94/design-language, channel stable, kind asset-bundle, dest vendor/aura) and run `recipe.py sync-deps` to land the bundle at vendor/aura (or use the git-submodule / static/aura build-step alternative). Config defaults (model-effort-profile, execution-strategy) are applied via the model-effort-profile-switch and workflow-variant-switch skills — not edited inline. Scaffolded templates/ (base.html, pages/index.html, fragments/example.html) are thin Grimoire-owned skeletons that include/reference Aura's artifacts; replace the include mechanism to match your template engine. A `justfile` + `compose.yaml` ship the idempotent, multi-worktree-safe Dockerized-Postgres `db-up` recipe (#81 — reuses a running shared container by exact name via `docker ps` filters, starts a stopped one, only `docker compose up`s when none exists; never Go-template `{{...}}` output which collides with just's `{{ }}`); edit `db_name`/`container_name` and the `run`/`test` bodies, and see the inline multi-worktree shared-container note near the DB recipes. Full guidance: docs/web-app-aura-adoption-guide.md.
